skill.name: vue-testing-best-practices
skill.description: Use this skill to design and troubleshoot Vue 3 tests with Vitest, Vue Test Utils, Pinia, async components, Teleport, and Playwright.
skill.summary: Improve Vue 3 test quality with practical guidance for component, composable, store, async, and end-to-end testing.
skill.author: github.com/vuejs-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many dynamic import, command, URL, environment, and weak-crypto patterns, but review shows they are documentation examples for Vue testing. The files contain Markdown guidance, local development commands, CI flag examples, and official documentation links; no prompt injection, malicious execution, credential exfiltration, or hidden network behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Dynamic imports are Vue async component examples
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The import() expressions appear inside Markdown examples that teach testing of defineAsyncComponent and Suspense. They load local Vue component paths in illustrative test code, not untrusted input or marketplace runtime code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are within documentation examples about Vue async components. No evidence shows dynamic import of user-controlled paths or execution by the skill itself.

low_findings.title: Command patterns are testing setup snippets
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The command-like content is standard Vue testing documentation, including package installation, package.json test scripts, and Playwright development commands. These examples may ask users to run normal project tooling, but they do not execute automatically or hide unsafe shell behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples use common npm, npx, Vitest, and Playwright commands for test setup. There is no evidence of command injection, remote script download, or obfuscated execution.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs are documentation links or localhost
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The URL findings point to official Vue, Vue Test Utils, Vitest, Playwright, Pinia, and local development server references. They are citations or localhost test configuration values, not outbound exfiltration endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible references or localhost values used by Playwright examples. No evidence found of requests that transmit user data or secrets.

low_findings.title: Environment access is limited to CI flags
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The process.env examples only inspect the CI flag to adjust retries, workers, server reuse, and Vitest threading. They do not read secret variables, environment files, tokens, or credentials.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only observed environment variable is process.env.CI in test configuration examples. No evidence found of .env file parsing, credential access, or network transmission.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptography findings are text-pattern matches
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The weak-crypto alerts align with frontmatter, impact descriptions, and ordinary Vue testing prose rather than cryptographic APIs. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, insecure random generation, or password hashing code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review found testing descriptions at the flagged locations, not cryptographic operations. A repository search found no evidence of cryptographic implementation code.

content.value_statement: Vue test suites often become flaky, brittle, or difficult to configure. This skill provides focused guidance for reliable Vue 3 tests using Vitest, Vue Test Utils, Pinia, async helpers, and Playwright.
actual_capabilities: Recommends Vitest configuration patterns for Vue 3 and Vite projects.
Explains behavior-focused component testing with Vue Test Utils.
Guides async testing with nextTick, flushPromises, Suspense, and dynamic components.
Shows Pinia testing setup for stores, actions, plugins, and component mounts.
Compares node, browser, and end-to-end runners for different Vue testing needs.
Provides Playwright setup guidance for Vue end-to-end testing.
limitations: Does not run tests or inspect project files automatically.
Focuses on Vue 3, Vite, Vitest, Vue Test Utils, Pinia, and Playwright workflows.
Example commands and configuration snippets may require adaptation for each repository.
Does not replace a full test strategy, accessibility audit, or security review.

use_cases.title: Stabilize flaky component tests
use_cases.description: Diagnose async update problems and choose the right waits for Vue DOM updates, promises, and user events.

use_cases.title: Plan Vue test infrastructure
use_cases.description: Select Vitest, browser mode, or Playwright based on test scope, runtime needs, and CI constraints.

use_cases.title: Review team testing patterns
use_cases.description: Replace brittle snapshots and implementation-aware checks with behavior-focused assertions and maintainable helpers.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Vue test runner
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose a Vue 3 testing setup for a Vite project. Compare Vitest, Vue Test Utils, browser mode, and Playwright for my use case.

prompt_templates.title: Fix an async test failure
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this failing Vue test and explain whether I should use nextTick, flushPromises, waitFor, or Suspense. Focus on the async timing issue.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Pinia test setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate my Pinia testing approach. Recommend when to use createTestingPinia, setActivePinia, stubbed actions, and real action execution.

prompt_templates.title: Design a testing strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Vue testing strategy for a large app with composables, Pinia stores, async components, Teleport modals, browser-specific behavior, and E2E flows.

output_examples.input: A component test fails before async data renders.
output_examples.output: The likely issue is unresolved promises after mounting. The recommendation is to await Vue updates, flush pending promises, and assert only after the async component or request has settled.

output_examples.input: A team relies on broad snapshots for component coverage.
output_examples.output: The response recommends smaller snapshots only for stable markup and adds behavior assertions for visible text, emitted events, form state, and user interactions.

output_examples.input: A modal rendered with Teleport cannot be found in wrapper queries.
output_examples.output: The response explains why wrapper-scoped queries miss teleported DOM and recommends stubbing Teleport for unit tests or querying document body for integration tests.

best_practices: Test user-visible behavior before implementation details.
Use the smallest runner that accurately covers the behavior under test.
Await Vue updates, external promises, and user events explicitly.
anti_patterns: Using snapshots as the only proof that a component works.
Calling private component methods instead of interacting through rendered output.
Using browser or E2E tests for logic that fast component tests can cover.

faq.question: What Vue versions does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets Vue 3 projects, especially applications built with Vite, Vitest, Vue Test Utils, Pinia, and Playwright.

faq.question: Can it help choose between Vitest and Playwright?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains when to use fast component tests, browser-based component tests, and end-to-end Playwright tests.

faq.question: Does it generate complete test files?
faq.answer: It can guide test structure and examples, but generated tests should be reviewed and adapted to the project codebase.

faq.question: Does it cover async Vue behavior?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers nextTick, flushPromises, Suspense, async setup, defineAsyncComponent, and timing-related test failures.

faq.question: Does it support Pinia testing?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers createTestingPinia, setActivePinia, action stubbing, real action execution, and store isolation.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: The audit found only documentation examples and normal testing references. No malicious behavior or prompt injection was identified.
